channel.title: Social Software on CEDIS
channel.description: Recent content in Social Software on CEDIS

item.title: Products
item.description: Mobile application developed as a bachelor’s thesis at the University of Brasilia to support the preservation and teaching of Brazilian Indigenous languages, initially focused on Munduruku, through translation lookup, history, and collaborative curation.

item.title: Social Software
item.description: Research area involving the development and analysis of digital technologies (especially software) with the explicit goal of generating positive social impact, without profit-making or direct commercial exploitation. This approach is collaborative, interdisciplinary, and people-centered.

item.title: Products
item.description: Android mobile app developed as a bachelor’s thesis with academic support from CEDIS for teaching data structures through augmented reality and gamification, featuring themed rooms, mini missions, and an achievement passport.

item.title: People
item.description: Associate Professor at the University of Brasília (UnB), working in the Software Engineering undergraduate program and the Graduate Program in Applied Computing with a focus on requirements, human aspects, and software engineering education.

item.title: Products
item.description: Technological product recorded in 2025 for child sexual abuse prevention through gamification and an educational application for children.

item.title: Products
item.description: 2025 technological product focused on preventing childhood violence through a gamified application for parents and educators.

item.title: Products
item.description: 2025 technological product focused on supporting mood, mental health, and well-being in the university environment.

item.title: People
item.description: Adjunct Professor at the University of Brasília (UnB), working in the undergraduate Software Engineering program on software engineering, software metrics, testing, and software quality.

item.title: Software Engineering students' thesis gives rise to Atloria, an augmented reality app for teaching data structures
item.description: A bachelor's thesis supervised by Prof. Sergio Freitas resulted in Atloria, an educational augmented reality app.
